steps.title: Count total numbers and identify negative values
steps.content: The data set contains three numbers: $-9$, $6$, and $18$. Among these, only $-9$ is negative. The other two values are positive. So we have 1 negative number out of 3 total numbers.

steps.title: Apply the probability formula
steps.content: Probability of selecting a negative number is: $$P(\text{negative}) = \frac{\text{Number of negative values}}{\text{Total number of values}} = \frac{1}{3}$$ That matches Choice B — $\frac{1}{3}$.
